title: Intensely Jammy Vietnamese Shrimp Mi Quang
description: A pescatarian-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring shrimp, white wine vinegar, and shio-cure, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This mi quang recipe is jammy and shattering, with a balance of flavors and textures.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat and add the shrimp, cooking until they are pink and fully cooked.
Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set them aside, leaving the oil behind.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the white wine vinegar, shio-cure, fish sauce, lime juice, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Return the cooked shrimp to the skillet and toss to coat with the sauce.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and toss to combine with the shrimp and sauce.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce has thickened and the noodles are well coated.
Serve the mi quang hot, garnished with additional chili flakes and lime wedges if desired.
